paragraphs.text: A small chemistry note: this absence is plausible because aspirin often hydrolyzes to salicylate + acetate in aqueous solution, while Pu(IV) also hydrolyzes strongly, making well-defined aqueous acetylsalicylate constants difficult to curate. Source: **NIST SRD-46**.

claims.text: aspirin often hydrolyzes to salicylate + acetate in aqueous solution
making well-defined aqueous acetylsalicylate constants difficult to curate.
